name: ChatGPT
short_description: AI chatbot for answering questions and helping with assignments
description: ChatGPT is an AI assistant that can help students with research, writing, coding, and problem-solving. It provides detailed explanations and can assist with homework across subjects.

name: Grammarly
short_description: AI writing assistant that checks grammar and improves essay clarity
description: Grammarly helps students write better essays and papers by checking grammar, spelling, punctuation, and style. It offers real-time suggestions to improve clarity and tone.

name: Quizlet
short_description: Study tool with flashcards and practice tests
description: Quizlet helps students memorize information through flashcards, practice tests, and study games. Perfect for exam prep and learning new concepts across any subject.

name: Notion
short_description: All-in-one workspace for notes, tasks, and project management
description: Notion combines note-taking, task management, and databases in one place. Students use it to organize class notes, track assignments, and manage group projects.

name: GitHub Copilot
short_description: AI coding assistant that suggests code as you type
description: GitHub Copilot helps students write code faster by suggesting completions and entire functions. It supports multiple programming languages and learns from context.

name: Canva
short_description: Easy graphic design tool for presentations and posters
description: Canva makes it simple to create professional presentations, posters, and graphics. Students can use templates for school projects and assignments without design experience.

name: Otter.ai
short_description: AI transcription tool for lectures and meetings
description: Otter.ai automatically transcribes lectures and meetings in real-time. Students can review transcripts, search for keywords, and share notes with classmates.

name: Wolfram Alpha
short_description: Computational knowledge engine for math and science
description: Wolfram Alpha solves complex math problems and provides step-by-step solutions. Essential for STEM students working on calculus, physics, and chemistry.

name: Zotero
short_description: Free reference manager for research papers
description: Zotero helps students collect, organize, and cite research sources. It automatically generates bibliographies in any citation style for papers and theses.

name: Hemingway Editor
short_description: Writing tool that makes your text clear and concise
description: Hemingway Editor highlights complex sentences and suggests simpler alternatives. Helps students write clearer essays and reports by improving readability.

name: Anki
short_description: Spaced repetition flashcard app for long-term memorization
description: Anki uses spaced repetition to help students memorize information efficiently. Popular for language learning, medical school, and exam preparation.

name: Overleaf
short_description: Online LaTeX editor for academic papers
description: Overleaf is a collaborative LaTeX editor perfect for writing research papers, theses, and technical documents. Real-time collaboration makes group projects easier.

name: Replit
short_description: Online code editor and IDE for learning programming
description: Replit lets students write and run code in the browser without setup. Supports 50+ languages and includes collaboration features for coding assignments.

name: Figma
short_description: Collaborative design tool for UI/UX projects
description: Figma is a web-based design tool for creating interfaces, prototypes, and graphics. Students can collaborate in real-time on design projects.

name: Todoist
short_description: Task manager for organizing assignments and deadlines
description: Todoist helps students track assignments, set deadlines, and prioritize tasks. Simple interface makes it easy to stay organized throughout the semester.

name: Google Scholar
short_description: Search engine for academic papers and research
description: Google Scholar helps students find peer-reviewed papers, theses, and academic articles. Essential for literature reviews and research projects.

name: Desmos
short_description: Free graphing calculator for math students
description: Desmos is an advanced graphing calculator that works in the browser. Perfect for visualizing functions, plotting data, and exploring mathematical concepts.

name: Duolingo
short_description: Free language learning app with gamification
description: Duolingo makes language learning fun through bite-sized lessons and games. Students can learn Spanish, French, German, and 30+ other languages.

name: Mendeley
short_description: Reference manager and academic social network
description: Mendeley helps students organize research papers, generate citations, and discover new research. Includes PDF annotation and cloud sync.

name: Trello
short_description: Visual project management with boards and cards
description: Trello uses boards and cards to organize projects and tasks. Students use it for group projects, assignment tracking, and personal organization.

name: Quillbot
short_description: AI paraphrasing tool for rewriting text
description: QuillBot helps students rephrase sentences and paragraphs while maintaining meaning. Useful for avoiding plagiarism and improving writing variety.

name: Codecademy
short_description: Interactive platform for learning to code
description: Codecademy offers interactive coding lessons in Python, JavaScript, HTML/CSS, and more. Students learn by writing real code in the browser.

name: Loom
short_description: Screen recording tool for video presentations
description: Loom makes it easy to record your screen and camera for video presentations. Students use it for project demos, tutorials, and remote collaboration.

name: Evernote
short_description: Note-taking app with web clipping and organization
description: Evernote helps students capture notes, clip web articles, and organize research. Syncs across devices for access anywhere.

name: Khan Academy
short_description: Free educational videos and practice exercises
description: Khan Academy offers free lessons in math, science, economics, and more. Students can learn at their own pace with videos and practice problems.

name: Perplexity AI
short_description: AI search engine with cited sources
description: Perplexity AI answers questions with real-time web search and provides citations. Helpful for research and fact-checking with transparent sources.

name: Notion AI
short_description: AI writing assistant built into Notion
description: Notion AI helps students brainstorm ideas, summarize notes, and improve writing directly in their Notion workspace. Integrated with existing notes and projects.

name: Slidesgo
short_description: Free presentation templates for students
description: Slidesgo offers thousands of free Google Slides and PowerPoint templates. Students can create professional presentations without starting from scratch.

name: Forest
short_description: Focus app that helps you stay off your phone
description: Forest helps students focus by gamifying phone-free time. Plant virtual trees while studying, and they die if you use your phone.

name: Mathway
short_description: Math problem solver with step-by-step solutions
description: Mathway solves math problems from basic algebra to calculus. Shows step-by-step solutions to help students understand the process.

name: Coursera
short_description: Online courses from universities and companies
description: Coursera offers thousands of courses from top universities. Students can learn new skills, earn certificates, and access many courses for free.

name: Grammarly GO
short_description: AI writing assistant with generative features
description: Grammarly GO combines grammar checking with AI writing help. Students can generate drafts, rewrite paragraphs, and get writing suggestions.

name: Excalidraw
short_description: Free hand-drawn style diagramming tool
description: Excalidraw creates simple diagrams and sketches with a hand-drawn aesthetic. Perfect for brainstorming, flowcharts, and visual explanations.

name: Obsidian
short_description: Knowledge base and note-taking with linking
description: Obsidian helps students build a personal knowledge base by linking notes together. Great for research, thesis writing, and connecting ideas.

name: Symbolab
short_description: Math solver with detailed step-by-step explanations
description: Symbolab solves algebra, calculus, and trigonometry problems with detailed explanations. Helps students learn math concepts through worked examples.

name: Speechify
short_description: Text-to-speech app for listening to documents
description: Speechify converts textbooks, articles, and PDFs into audio. Students can listen while commuting or multitasking to save time.

name: Miro
short_description: Online whiteboard for brainstorming and collaboration
description: Miro is a digital whiteboard for visual collaboration. Students use it for group brainstorming, mind mapping, and project planning.

name: Stack Overflow
short_description: Q&A community for programming help
description: Stack Overflow is where programmers ask and answer coding questions. Essential resource for students learning to code and debugging errors.

name: Scribbr
short_description: Citation generator and plagiarism checker
description: Scribbr generates citations in APA, MLA, Chicago, and other styles. Also offers plagiarism checking and proofreading services for students.

name: Calendly
short_description: Scheduling tool for meetings and office hours
description: Calendly makes it easy to schedule meetings without email back-and-forth. Students use it for group project meetings and professor office hours.

name: Notion Calendar
short_description: Calendar integrated with Notion workspace
description: Notion Calendar connects your schedule with your Notion notes and tasks. Students can see assignments, deadlines, and events in one place.

name: Deepl
short_description: AI translator with natural-sounding results
description: DeepL provides more natural translations than other tools. Helpful for language students and reading foreign research papers.

name: Photopea
short_description: Free online photo editor like Photoshop
description: Photopea is a free browser-based image editor with Photoshop-like features. Students can edit photos and create graphics without expensive software.

name: Sci-Hub
short_description: Access to research papers (use ethically)
description: Sci-Hub provides free access to millions of research papers. Note: Check your institution's library first and use responsibly.

name: Hemingway App
short_description: Writing tool for clear and bold prose
description: Hemingway App highlights complex sentences and passive voice. Helps students write more clearly and concisely for better grades.

name: Clockify
short_description: Free time tracking for productivity
description: Clockify helps students track how much time they spend on assignments and projects. Useful for improving time management and productivity.

name: Brilliant
short_description: Interactive learning for math and science
description: Brilliant teaches math, science, and computer science through interactive problem-solving. Great for students who want to deepen their understanding.

name: Kami
short_description: PDF annotation and collaboration tool
description: Kami lets students annotate PDFs, collaborate on documents, and fill out forms. Integrates with Google Classroom and other learning platforms.

name: Soundtrap
short_description: Online music and podcast creation studio
description: Soundtrap is a browser-based audio editor for creating music and podcasts. Students can collaborate in real-time on audio projects.

name: Removebg
short_description: AI tool to remove image backgrounds instantly
description: Remove.bg uses AI to remove backgrounds from images in seconds. Useful for presentations, design projects, and social media graphics.
